executeHooks((address,uint256,bytes,bool.details: Will deploy and initialize the user proxy at a deterministic address      if one doesn't already exist.

params.user: - User to set ens records for.
- User to deploy the proxy for.
params.withEns: - whether to initialize the ens or not

addr(bytes32).notice: eth address for the given node.

forwardResolutionNodeToAddress(bytes32).notice: maps the subnode label hash(`<user>`) to the proxy address

initializeEns(address).notice: initialize the ens records for given user proxy.

initializeProxy(address,bool).notice: deploy user proxy if not already deployed, optionally even setup the ens records.         if any user wants to opt-out of ens to save on gas, they need to initialize the proxy         prior to first hooks execution.

name(bytes32).notice: Reverse resolution name for given node.

ownerOf(address).notice: mapping of proxy address to owner address.

proxyOf(address).notice: returns the address where the user proxy will get deployed. It is deterministic         deployment with create2.

reverseResolutionNodeToAddress(bytes32).notice: maps the `<proxy-address>.<base-name>` node to the user address
